parameters.jsCode:
/**
 * AI Talent Copilot — Screening Engine demo batch
 * Replace this payload with your own JD + CV texts (or load from files).
 * Fictional profiles only — see /demo in the repo.
 */
return [{
  json: {
    job_title: 'Senior Backend Java Engineer',
    job_description: [
      'Title: Senior Backend Java Engineer',
      'Seniority: Senior (6+ years)',
      '',
      'Hard requirements:',
      '- Java 17+',
      '- Spring Boot',
      '- Microservices',
      '- AWS (ECS/EKS, SQS/SNS)',
      '- Apache Kafka (or strong event-driven experience)',
      '- SQL (PostgreSQL/MySQL)',
      '- CI/CD and automated testing',
      '',
      'Preferred: Leadership / tech lead, Observability, Mentoring',
      'Experience: 6+ years backend, 3+ years production microservices',
      'Responsibilities: Design Spring Boot services, own Kafka pipelines, mentor engineers'
    ].join('\n'),
    candidates: [
      {
        candidate_name: 'Alex Rivera',
        cv_text: [
          'Alex Rivera — Backend engineer, 8 years Java microservices on AWS. Led squad of 5.',
          'NovaPayments (2021–Present) Tech Lead/Senior Backend:',
          '- 12 Spring Boot microservices (Java 17) on AWS EKS',
          '- Kafka pipelines ~40M messages/day; p99 latency -35%',
          '- Mentored 4 engineers; hiring loops',
          'CloudRetail (2018–2021): monolith to microservices; PostgreSQL; SQS/SNS',
          'DataBridge (2016–2018): Spring Boot REST + MySQL',
          'Skills: Java, Spring Boot, Microservices, AWS, Kafka, PostgreSQL, MySQL, CI/CD, mentoring'
        ].join('\n')
      },
      {
        candidate_name: 'Jordan Lee',
        cv_text: [
          'Jordan Lee — Backend developer, 5 years Java/Spring Boot.',
          'ShopWave (2022–Present): Spring Boot APIs, PostgreSQL, Redis, AWS ECS, JUnit',
          'MediSoft (2020–2022): Spring Boot tools + MySQL batch jobs',
          'SoftLabs (2019–2020): Java monolith bugfixes',
          'Skills: Java, Spring Boot, SQL, AWS ECS (basic), REST, JUnit',
          'Gaps: no production Kafka; under 6 years; no leadership evidence'
        ].join('\n')
      },
      {
        candidate_name: 'Sam Okonkwo',
        cv_text: [
          'Sam Okonkwo — EM / former Senior Backend, 10 years Java. Leads platform team of 8.',
          'Finora EM Platform (2022–Present): Java platform libs; microservices AWS ECS+RDS; hiring scorecards',
          'Finora Senior Backend (2019–2022): ledger services; SQS + Kafka topics; mentoring',
          'LogiCore (2015–2019): Java logistics services; SQL reporting',
          'Skills: Java, Spring Boot, Microservices, AWS, Kafka (prior), SQL, CI/CD, engineering management'
        ].join('\n')
      }
    ]
  }
}];

assignments.name: eval_prompt
assignments.value:
=You are the Screening Engine of AI Talent Copilot.

Task: evaluate ONE candidate against ONE job description.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown fences, no prose outside JSON).

Scoring rubric (0–100 integers):
- technical_match: required technologies / stack depth
- experience_match: years, seniority, domain similarity
- leadership_match: people leadership, mentoring, ownership (0 if not required and no evidence)
- overall_score: weighted — technical 45%, experience 35%, leadership 20%.
  If JD does not require leadership: technical 55%, experience 45%, leadership 0.

Anti-bias rules:
- Ignore names as demographic signals.
- Score only skills, experience, and JD requirements.
- Do not invent employers, dates, or skills.
- Optional nice-to-haves are not weighted like hard requirements.

interview_priority:
- HIGH: overall_score >= 80
- MEDIUM: 60–79
- LOW: < 60

JSON schema:
{
  "candidate_name": "string",
  "overall_score": 0,
  "technical_match": 0,
  "experience_match": 0,
  "leadership_match": 0,
  "strengths": ["string"],
  "gaps": ["string"],
  "recommendation": "string",
  "interview_priority": "HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW"
}

JOB TITLE:
{{ $json.job_title }}

JOB DESCRIPTION:
{{ $json.job_description }}

CANDIDATE NAME:
{{ $json.candidate_name }}

CANDIDATE CV:
{{ $json.cv_text }}

nodes.name: Optional JD RAG (jobs_knowledge)
nodes.notes: Enable after running workflow 04. Use retrieved docs to enrich job_description.
